terms.definition: A nation-state possessing unmatched military, economic, and cultural influence on the global stage.
A political system combining representative government with constitutionally protected civil liberties and market economics.
A packet-switched, globally routable network based on TCP/IP protocols enabling end-to-end data exchange.
A socially or technologically enforced limit on when and how a person can be reached or stimulated.
Any unsolicited or low-value signal that reduces the effective channel capacity for high-value information.
An index measuring the degree to which individuals in a society align with and act upon shared normative signals.
An abrupt qualitative change in macroscopic behaviour resulting from continuous variation of an underlying parameter.
A regime in which systemic coherence has collapsed to a low equilibrium but remains dynamically steady rather than further declining.
The social process by which behaviours once considered deviant become accepted as standard background practices.
A parameter governing the speed at which social coherence approaches its low-equilibrium value in the differential model dχ/dt = –λ(χ – χ*).
